source_target: Agreement between the City of Austin and Austin EMS Association (AEMSA) -- Meet and Confer Agreement, 2023-2027
reason: Downloaded and text-extracted (88 pages, 250,194 chars, clean text layer via ingest/extract_text.py). Confirms a genuine, complete meet-and-confer agreement (Article 1 Preamble through Article 26, plus wage-schedule appendices) between the City of Austin and the Austin EMS Association, on the same official austintexas.gov/labor-relations page family and austin.widen.net hosting pattern already used for the ingested Austin police and fire agreements. Article 3 Recognition: 'The CITY recognizes the ASSOCIATION as the sole and exclusive bargaining agent for all Uniformed Staff.' Article 2 Definitions confirms 'Uniformed Staff' applies only to employees 'Employed in the Department as "Emergency Medical Services Personnel" as defined by Texas Health and Safety Code, Chapter 773' and 'Whose position requires substantial knowledge of "Emergency Prehospital Care"' -- explicitly excluding civilian employees. Article 6 sets a real 4-year fiscal-year base wage schedule (FY23-24 baseline; FY24-25 +4.0%; FY25-26 +3.0%; FY26-27 +3.0%) plus education incentive pay. Article 23 confirms the term runs from ratification or October 1, 2023 (whichever is later) through September 30, 2027.
caveat: Austin-Travis County EMS is a separate department from the Austin Fire Department, governed by its own Chapter 142 Subchapter D meet-and-confer statute and a joint Firefighters'/Police Officers'/EMS Civil Service Commission under Chapter 143 -- i.e., EMS is civil-service-protected and statutorily adjacent to police/fire, but is NOT itself 'police' or 'fire' under this project's controlled occupation_class vocabulary. Classified as occupation_class=nurse_health (closest schema fit for pre-hospital emergency medical personnel), safety_flag=0, consistent with this project's existing practice of coding safety-adjacent occupations (dispatchers, nurses) as legitimate non-safety comparison units. This is a single-occupation bargaining unit (EMS personnel only, civilians explicitly excluded) -- no recognition-clause-first bundling ambiguity applies.
notes: Resolves the Austin non-safety held-out target that a prior session left open after confirming AFSCME Local 1624's document was a consultation policy, not a wage-setting agreement. A July 18, 2024 amendment to this agreement also exists at the same official page; reviewed for context but not fetched as a separate document, since the base 2023-2027 agreement is a complete, self-sufficient text.

source_target: City Council item File #26-1362 (vacation-leave-accrual boilerplate referencing 'non-sworn employees not covered by collective bargaining or meet and confer agreements')
reason: Downloaded and read in full (2,328 chars, clean text layer); an unrelated City Clerk compensation item that only generically distinguishes 'non-sworn employees not covered by collective bargaining or meet and confer agreements' from those who are (i.e., police/fire/EMS) -- it does not identify a separate civilian non-safety bargaining agreement and is not itself a wage-setting source.
caveat: A dead-end lead that nonetheless helped confirm no additional non-safety bargaining channel exists beyond EMS.
notes: Read for verification only; not stored in corpus/.
